description: Death of Prophet Muḥammad and election of Abū Bakr
key_events: Prophet dies 12 Rabīʿ I 11 AH (8 June 632) | Saqīfa meeting - Abū Bakr elected caliph | News spreads to Arabian tribes
name: Succession Crisis

commanders: Qays ibn Makshūḥ | Fayrūz al-Daylamī | Local Yemeni chiefs
description: Suppression of al-Aswad al-ʿAnsī's movement in Yemen
key_events: al-Aswad claims prophethood (late 10 AH) | al-Aswad conquers Yemen, kills Persian governor Bādhām's son | Assassination plot by Qays ibn Makshūḥ and Fayrūz al-Daylamī | al-Aswad killed in Ṣanʿāʾ (night before Prophet's death or shortly after)
name: Yemen Campaign
outcome: al-Aswad assassinated; Yemen returned to Islamic authority

description: Initial defense against tribes threatening Medina
key_events: Tribal delegations demand return of ṣadaqa (alms) | Abū Bakr refuses: 'By God, if they withhold a she-kid...' | Bedouin tribes gather at Dhū l-Qaṣṣa | Abū Bakr leads defense, defeats raiders at Dhū l-Qaṣṣa
name: Defense of Medina
opponents: ʿAbs | Dhubyān | elements of Asad and Ghaṭafān

description: Campaign against Ṭulayḥa and allied tribes in central Arabia
key_events: Khālid ibn al-Walīd appointed commander | Battle of Buzākha - Asad and Ghaṭafān defeated | Ṭulayḥa flees to Syria (later converts back to Islam) | ʿUyayna ibn Ḥiṣn captured then released
name: Najd Campaign (Buzākha)
opponents: Ṭulayḥa ibn Khuwaylid | Banū Asad | Ghaṭafān | Banū Fazāra
outcome: Decisive Muslim victory; Ṭulayḥa's movement collapses

controversy: Mālik's execution debated - was he Muslim or apostate?
key_events: Khālid moves against Banū Tamīm elements | Mālik ibn Nuwayra refuses to pay zakāt | Execution of Mālik ibn Nuwayra (controversial) | Khālid marries Mālik's widow (scandal) | ʿUmar demands Khālid's punishment; Abū Bakr refuses
name: Tamīm Campaign (al-Biṭāḥ)
opponents: Mālik ibn Nuwayra | elements of Banū Tamīm

casualties: ~1,200 Muslims killed (including many ḥuffāẓ); ~10,000+ Ḥanīfa killed
commanders: Khālid ibn al-Walīd | Shurahbīl ibn Ḥasana | ʿIkrima ibn Abī Jahl (initial)
description: Major campaign against Musaylima and Banū Ḥanīfa
key_events: Initial Muslim defeat at ʿAqrabāʾ (first engagement) | Reinforcements arrive under Khālid | Battle of ʿAqrabāʾ (main battle) | Garden of Death (Ḥadīqat al-Mawt) - final stand | Musaylima killed by Waḥshī ibn Ḥarb | Heavy casualties among ḥuffāẓ (Quran memorizers)
name: Yamāma Campaign
opponents: Musaylima ibn Ḥabīb | Banū Ḥanīfa | Mujjāʿa ibn Murāra
outcome: Decisive Muslim victory; Musaylima killed; Ḥanīfa submit
significance: Largest and most important battle of Ridda Wars

description: Suppression of rebellion in eastern Arabia
key_events: al-Ḥuṭam ibn Ḍubayʿa leads rebellion | Siege of Jawāthā | al-ʿAlāʾ ibn al-Ḥaḍramī's campaign | ʿAbd al-Qays remain loyal to Islam | Bakr ibn Wāʾil elements rebel then submit
name: Bahrain Campaign
opponents: al-Ḥuṭam ibn Ḍubayʿa | elements of Bakr ibn Wāʾil

commanders: Ḥudhayfa ibn Miḥṣan | ʿIkrima ibn Abī Jahl
key_events: Laqīṭ ibn Mālik Dhū l-Tāj claims kingship | Battle of Dibā | Laqīṭ killed | Azd of Oman submit
name: Oman Campaign
opponents: Laqīṭ ibn Mālik Dhū l-Tāj | Azd of Oman

commanders: Ziyād ibn Labīd | al-Muhājir ibn Abī Umayya
description: Final campaign against Kinda in Ḥaḍramawt
key_events: Kinda under al-Ashʿath ibn Qays rebel | Siege of al-Nujayr fortress | al-Ashʿath captured attempting to flee | al-Ashʿath pardoned, marries Abū Bakr's sister | al-Ashʿath later becomes Muslim commander
name: Ḥaḍramawt Campaign
